external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes. Automated commits only. No substantive model updates.

critical_finding.title: sections.json NEVER INJECTED — SECTIONS_AVAILABLE circular dependency bug
critical_finding.description: generate-html.js line 1777 checks 'if (SECTIONS_AVAILABLE)' but SECTIONS_AVAILABLE starts as false and is ONLY set to true INSIDE loadSectionsCache(). loadSectionsCache() is only called within the block that requires SECTIONS_AVAILABLE to be true. Circular dependency: sections.json is NEVER injected into the HTML. All sections.json prose improvements (EXP-001 through EXP-012, all analyst expansions, all prose patches) are silently discarded on every build.
critical_finding.evidence: node -e shows SECTIONS_AVAILABLE=false before check. Manual simulation of integrateSection DOES work correctly. The bug is purely the guard condition.
critical_finding.fix: In generate-html.js, change line 1777: 'if (SECTIONS_AVAILABLE) { const sections = loadSectionsCache();' to 'const sections = loadSectionsCache(); if (sections) {'. One-line fix. All sections.json content will begin appearing in the HTML immediately.
critical_finding.impact: All prose improvements to sections.json are invisible in the live site. The HTML has been showing only hardcoded generate-html.js template content since V4.9.7 (when sections.json was first extracted).

internal_issues.description: SECTIONS_AVAILABLE circular dependency — sections.json never injected into HTML build

suggested_patch.description: Change guard condition from 'if (SECTIONS_AVAILABLE) { const sections = loadSectionsCache();' to 'const sections = loadSectionsCache(); if (sections) {' — requires human to apply to infrastructure file
